13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer is the SD-024 Stage 1 behavioral
17	// proof that the server dispatch path at runWorker carries
18	// review_max_retries from .ddx/config.yaml through to the running loop.
19	//
20	// The test mirrors the production wiring introduced in workers.go: it
21	// drives StartExecuteLoop → runWorker against a real on-disk
22	// .ddx/config.yaml (the same file the runWorker dispatch site's
23	// config.LoadAndResolve call reads). The deterministic review-failure
24	// fixture from testfixtures provides the executor + reviewer pair via
25	// BeadWorkerFactory so behavior is observable end-to-end without a real
26	// agent harness.
27	//
28	// Configured values:
29	//   - .ddx/config.yaml: review_max_retries: 5
30	//   - fixture: FailUntilCall=4 (attempts 1-4 return reviewer error,
31	//     attempt 5 returns APPROVE)
32	//
33	// Expected behavior:
34	//   - 5 StartExecuteLoop iterations drive 5 reviewer attempts.
35	//   - attempts 1-4 emit review-error events.
36	//   - attempt 5 emits an APPROVE review event and the loop closes
37	//     the bead via CloseWithEvidence.
38	//   - no review-manual-required event fires (threshold=5 ≥ attempts=5).
39	func TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer(t *testing.T) {
40		const (
41			failUntil = 4
42			threshold = 5
43			beadID    = "ddx-server-rmr-001"
44			fixedRev  = "cafebabe00112233"
45		)
46	
47		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
48		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
49		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
50	
51		// Real on-disk .ddx/config.yaml — this is the file the server
52		// dispatch path's config.LoadAndResolve call reads. The presence of
53		// review_max_retries: 5 here is the entire premise of the test.
54		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
55	library:
56	  path: ./library
57	  repository:
58	    url: https://github.com/example/repo
59	    branch: main
60	review_max_retries: 5
61	`
62		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
63	
64		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
65		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
66		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "server e2e review-retry threshold", Priority: 0}))
67	
68		runner := &testfixtures.ReviewFailureRunner{
69			ResultRev:     fixedRev,
70			FailUntilCall: failUntil,
71		}
72	
73		m := NewWorkerManager(projectRoot)
74		defer m.StopWatchdog()
75		// Inject the deterministic executor+reviewer pair through the same
76		// factory seam runWorker honours in production. The factory bypasses
77		// the real agent runner so behavior is fully observable.
78		m.BeadWorkerFactory = func(s agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore) *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker {
79			return &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
80				Store:    s,
81				Executor: runner.Executor(),
82				Reviewer: runner.Reviewer(),
83			}
84		}
85	
86		// Drive failUntil + 1 iterations through StartExecuteLoop → runWorker
87		// (one --once invocation per attempt, matching the production loop's
88		// per-iteration claim/release rhythm). Between iterations the test
89		// unclaims the bead because the loop intentionally leaves it claimed
90		// on a reviewer-error iteration.
91		const totalIterations = failUntil + 1
92		for i := 0; i < totalIterations; i++ {
93			rec, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
94				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
95				Once:        true,
96			})
97			require.NoErrorf(t, err, "iteration %d: StartExecuteLoop", i+1)
98			_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, rec.ID, 10*time.Second)
99			if i < totalIterations-1 {
100				require.NoErrorf(t, store.Unclaim(beadID), "iteration %d: unclaim", i+1)

40	
41	// setupTestDir creates a temp directory with a library and bead store.
42	// Isolates the server's persistent state (XDG_DATA_HOME) to a per-test temp
43	// dir so tests don't accumulate registered projects in the user's real state
44	// file. Tests that need to assert on the state file location should read
45	// os.Getenv("XDG_DATA_HOME") after calling setupTestDir rather than setting
46	// their own value before.
47	func setupTestDir(t *testing.T) string {
48		t.Helper()
49		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", t.TempDir())
50		dir := t.TempDir()
51	
52		// Create .ddx/config.yaml so the server can find the library
53		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
54		if err := os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755); err != nil {
55			t.Fatal(err)
56		}
57		configYAML := `version: "1.0"
58	library:
59	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
60	  repository:
61	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
62	    branch: "main"
63	`
64		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(configYAML), 0o644); err != nil {
65			t.Fatal(err)
66		}
67	
68		// Create library with sample documents
69		libDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "plugins", "ddx")

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(server): wire-in subprocess integration test for worker register endpoint [ddx-2892f2cf]

Adds TestServerIngestion_RealWorkerCanRegister, which exercises the
production HTTP path (httptest.Server wrapping srv.Handler() — same mux,
same requireTrusted gate, real TCP socket) by re-execing the test binary
as a subprocess that POSTs /api/workers/register over the network. The
parent then asserts the in-memory registry shows the worker, proving the
round-trip from a real external HTTP client lands in the worker view.

Satisfies AC #6 of ADR-022 step 1.
EOF
)"

production HTTP path (httptest.Server wrapping srv.Handler() — same mux,
same requireTrusted gate, real TCP socket) by re-execing the test binary
as a subprocess that POSTs /api/workers/register over the network. The
parent then asserts the in-memory registry shows the worker, proving the
round-trip from a real external HTTP client lands in the worker view.

Satisfies AC #6 of ADR-022 step 1.
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. Pre-commit (incl. -race tests) passed.

Added `TestServerIngestion_RealWorkerCanRegister` (+ subprocess helper `TestHelperPostRegisterChild`) to `cli/internal/server/worker_ingest_test.go`. Parent test stands up `httptest.NewServer(srv.Handler())` and re-execs the test binary as a real subprocess that POSTs `/api/workers/register` over TCP; parent then asserts the in-memory registry shows the worker (worker_id, harness, host, RegisteredAt). Satisfies AC #6; full server suite green under `-race`.
